title: Dataset: EEG-Controlled Exoskeleton for Walking and Standing - A Longitudinal Study of Healthy Individuals
description: This longitudinal brain-machine interface study presents multimodal neurophysiological and kinematic data from seven healthy adults performing motor imagery tasks to control a lower-limb exoskeleton. The dataset comprises 60-channel EEG with EOG, dual inertial measurement units, and exoskeleton control signals collected across nine sessions per participant. Data include both open-loop calibration phases and closed-loop BMI control trials, providing a comprehensive resource for investigating neural correlates of motor imagery and human-machine interaction in ambulatory tasks.
methods_description: Seven healthy adults (ages 20–30 years) completed nine sessions of EEG-based brain-machine interface training and closed-loop exoskeleton control. Each session consisted of a motor imagery calibration phase followed by 12 closed-loop trials with walk/stop conditions. Extended motor imagery tasks (6-minute walk and stop imagery) were performed after trial blocks. Data acquisition included 60-channel scalp EEG with 4 EOG channels, 3-axis IMU sensors mounted on the forehead and exoskeleton, and continuous logging of decoder signals and BMI feedback.

funding_references.funder_name: UH–Methodist Graduate Fellowship for Translational Research
